pki.path.validate.reason: The RFC 5280 §6 path-validation surface is broad and still hardening — an algorithm-confusion defect in the one-shot PQC/EdDSA verify was fixed at 0.1.21 — and it is not yet interop-proven against an independent path validator in the integration harness. Graduate once the verify surface has stabilized and an external validator cross-checks a path it accepts/rejects.

pki.path.crlChecker.reason: Rides the same SIG_ALGS verify bridge as pki.path.validate (the algorithm-confusion fix touched both) and the same not-yet-interop-proven revocation path. Graduate alongside pki.path.validate.

pki.schema.attrcert.parse.reason: The RFC 5755 attribute-certificate parser is not yet interop-proven — no OpenSSL/NSS round-trip covers it in the integration harness — so its return shape is not frozen. It is part of the attribute-cert / CRMF / PKCS#12 / CMP format cohort the schema.all and schema.parse graduation waits on. Graduate once an independent implementation validates a structure it decodes.

pki.schema.attrcert.pemDecode.reason: Rides the same attribute-certificate parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no OpenSSL/NSS interop round-trip in the harness). Graduate alongside pki.schema.attrcert.parse.

pki.schema.all.reason: The format enumerator routes to the still-experimental attribute-cert, CRMF, PKCS#12, and CMP parsers; marking it stable would freeze their return shapes and continued registration into a stable contract. Graduate once every routed format is stable.

pki.schema.parse.reason: The auto-routing dispatcher's stable contract would include the still-experimental attribute-cert, CRMF, PKCS#12, and CMP return shapes it routes to, so an allowed change to any of those would break callers of a stable schema.parse. Graduate once every routed format is stable.

pki.schema.crmf.parse.reason: The RFC 4211 CertReqMessages parser is not yet interop-proven against an independent CRMF/CMP implementation in the integration harness, so its return shape is not frozen. Part of the same format cohort schema.all / schema.parse graduation waits on. Graduate once an external implementation validates a request it decodes.

pki.schema.crmf.pemDecode.reason: Rides the same CRMF parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no cross-implementation interop round-trip in the harness). Graduate alongside pki.schema.crmf.parse.

pki.schema.engine.encode.reason: The schema engine's encode direction backs the round-trip tests but has no external consumer that pins its API; the combinator surface is still absorbing new leaves (implicitBoolean landed at 0.1.23). Graduate once the combinator set is settled and an operator-facing encode path depends on it.

pki.schema.engine.embeddedDer.reason: The embedded-DER leaf is part of the still-settling engine combinator surface (implicitBoolean landed at 0.1.23); it has no external consumer pinning its return shape. Graduate alongside pki.schema.engine.encode once the leaf set is settled.

pki.schema.pkcs12.parse.reason: The RFC 7292 PKCS#12 parser is not yet interop-proven — no OpenSSL/NSS round-trip covers a PFX it decodes in the integration harness — so its return shape is not frozen. It is one of the format cohort (attribute-cert, CRMF, PKCS#12, CMP) that schema.all / schema.parse graduation waits on. Graduate once an independent implementation validates a store it decodes.

pki.schema.pkcs12.pemDecode.reason: Rides the same PKCS#12 parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no OpenSSL/NSS interop round-trip in the harness). Graduate alongside pki.schema.pkcs12.parse.

pki.schema.pkcs12.pemEncode.reason: Rides the same PKCS#12 parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no OpenSSL/NSS interop round-trip in the harness). Graduate alongside pki.schema.pkcs12.parse.
